---
schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: e0e68e2bf37f2c309c0b6943e6d998bed40106a7
findings:
  - { id: judged-judged-unmerged-branch-only-silently-omitted, kind: judged, text: "AC-1 promises 'never a silent omission' and its witness promises the four unmerged close/* branches (attest-helper, close-preflight, disposition-verb, home-status-glance) each appear 'kept as unmerged' — but the implemented row model (predicate.go Compute, per dc-2) draws branch-only rows exclusively from residue.Result.MergedBranches, which residue.Scan pre-filters to merged branches only. An UNMERGED branch with no worktree therefore appears in neither row shape and is silently absent from both the dry-run plan and the --apply report — no 'kept: unmerged' line ever prints for it, contradicting AC-1's witness sentence. Structural, still present at HEAD e0e68e2; foreclosed in-story by co-2 (residue frozen) and dc-1 (no git re-derivation). Matches ledger R4-I-82 adjudication (1), ACCEPTED-DEVIATION with the AC-text inconsistency controller-owned; safety intact (unmerged is never touched, merely never listed). Restated here because the in-repo living deviation-report for spec/gc-reclaim does not yet exist at .verdi/specs/active/gc-reclaim/ on this HEAD, so this disclosure currently lives only in workspace PLAN-V1.md. (confidence 0.55)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "ACCEPTED-DEVIATION per controller adjudication R4-I-82(1): AC-1's witness sentence promises 'kept: unmerged' lines for the four close/* branches, but the spec's own row model draws branch-only rows from residue's merged-only list -- the witness text contradicts the operative predicate two sentences earlier; the controller accepted that spec text at review, so the miss is the controller's, not this story's implementation. Fix is structurally foreclosed in-story: co-2 freezes internal/residue.Result byte-for-byte, and dc-1 forbids re-deriving eligibility from git independently. Safety intact: unmerged means never touched, merely never listed. Future work: a residue-v2 story exposing all-branch rows would make the disclosure buildable." }
  - { id: judged-judged-dc2-managed-step-widened-beyond-frozen-spec-text, kind: judged, text: "The frozen spec's dc-2 defines the managed exclusion as exactly 'Managed false (else kept: managed)' — residue's own field, resolved against the invoking checkout's WorktreesRoot. The shipped predicate (predicate.go classifyWorktreeRow, commit c4a9122 / PR #191) instead tests 'wt.Managed || looksManagedAnywhere(wt.Path)', adding a path-segment heuristic (any '/.verdi/data/worktrees/' component, derived from wtmanager.WorktreesRoot) so a worktree managed by ANOTHER checkout is kept rather than reclaimed. This is the R4-I-82 FIX disposition and is safety-tightening, arguably truer to AC-1's intent ('verdi gc's existing managed-worktree jurisdiction') than dc-2's letter — but the frozen spec text was never amended, so dc-2's ordered switch and AC-1's literal 'eligible iff' predicate no longer describe the shipped classification (some rows AC-1's literal reading calls eligible are now kept: managed, and a user-placed unmanaged worktree whose path merely contains the segment is conservatively mislabeled 'managed'). Needs recording in the story's deviation-report or a ratified dc-2 amendment; the current in-code comment is the only in-repo disclosure. (confidence 0.35)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "ACCEPTED-DEVIATION per controller adjudication R4-I-83: this divergence IS the R4-I-82(2) controller-adjudicated safety fix (PR #191's looksManagedAnywhere guard) -- its direction is strictly conservative, reclassifying rows eligible to kept only, never the reverse, so it cannot cause a deletion the frozen dc-2 text would forbid, only prevent one the frozen text would have wrongly allowed. Disclosed in code with citations to the finding and ledger; dc-2's frozen text remains accurate for the ordered structure and every other arm. Post-freeze divergence introduced by an adjudicated defect fix is the deviation mechanism's designed case; a spec amendment would be ceremony without safety value here." }
  - { id: judged-judged-dry-run-plan-omits-tip-commits, kind: judged, text: "The spec's Outcome prose promises 'Every reclaim-eligible LOCAL branch and its worktree (if any) is named with its tip commit', and AC-1's witness names tips for every listed branch — but dry-run output (report.go: KindEligible renders 'eligible: worktree \u003cpath\u003e + branch \u003cname\u003e') carries no tip; Row.Tip is populated only for KindReclaimed at --apply time (DeleteMergedBranch's pre-delete resolve). AC-2's operative dry-run sentence requires only path+branch, so the implementation conforms to the AC while the outcome prose overpromises; the recovery affordance exists only where recovery is actually needed (actual deletion). Matches ledger R4-I-82 adjudication (3), ACCEPTED-DEVIATION (prose-vs-AC looseness); restated because the in-repo living deviation-report is absent at this HEAD. (confidence 0.30)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "ACCEPTED-DEVIATION per controller adjudication R4-I-82(3): the judge itself notes AC-2's own operative dry-run text (path+branch only) is conformant; the outcome clause's prose overpromised ('named with its tip commit' for every eligible item). Disclosed as prose-vs-AC looseness, not a predicate defect -- tips print on actual deletion (the --apply path), exactly where the recovery affordance is needed." }
digest: sha256:162d6f4cc2606c0cead43fde182cc8a93dfc3570e4478feb5a3f98311fc3bd49
integrity: sha256:f60943b8efb9e26b34971797f0c47d4797fd953362e16203125e9eec4e0e5a1e
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "```json\n{\n  \"findings\": [\n    {\n      \"id\": \"judged-unmerged-branch-only-silently-omitted\",\n      \"text\": \"AC-1 promises 'never a silent omission' and its witness promises the four unmerged close/* branches (attest-helper, close-preflight, disposition-verb, home-status-glance) each appear 'kept as unmerged' — but the implemented row model (predicate.go Compute, per dc-2) draws branch-only rows exclusively from residue.Result.MergedBranches, which residue.Scan pre-filters to merged branches only. An UNMERGED branch with no worktree therefore appears in neither row shape and is silently absent from both the dry-run plan and the --apply report — no 'kept: unmerged' line ever prints for it, contradicting AC-1's witness sentence. Structural, still present at HEAD e0e68e2; foreclosed in-story by co-2 (residue frozen) and dc-1 (no git re-derivation). Matches ledger R4-I-82 adjudication (1), ACCEPTED-DEVIATION with the AC-text inconsistency controller-owned; safety intact (unmerged is never touched, merely never listed). Restated here because the in-repo living deviation-report for spec/gc-reclaim does not yet exist at .verdi/specs/active/gc-reclaim/ on this HEAD, so this disclosure currently lives only in workspace PLAN-V1.md.\",\n      \"confidence\": 0.55\n    },\n    {\n      \"id\": \"judged-dc2-managed-step-widened-beyond-frozen-spec-text\",\n      \"text\": \"The frozen spec's dc-2 defines the managed exclusion as exactly 'Managed false (else kept: managed)' — residue's own field, resolved against the invoking checkout's WorktreesRoot. The shipped predicate (predicate.go classifyWorktreeRow, commit c4a9122 / PR #191) instead tests 'wt.Managed || looksManagedAnywhere(wt.Path)', adding a path-segment heuristic (any '/.verdi/data/worktrees/' component, derived from wtmanager.WorktreesRoot) so a worktree managed by ANOTHER checkout is kept rather than reclaimed. This is the R4-I-82 FIX disposition and is safety-tightening, arguably truer to AC-1's intent ('verdi gc's existing managed-worktree jurisdiction') than dc-2's letter — but the frozen spec text was never amended, so dc-2's ordered switch and AC-1's literal 'eligible iff' predicate no longer describe the shipped classification (some rows AC-1's literal reading calls eligible are now kept: managed, and a user-placed unmanaged worktree whose path merely contains the segment is conservatively mislabeled 'managed'). Needs recording in the story's deviation-report or a ratified dc-2 amendment; the current in-code comment is the only in-repo disclosure.\",\n      \"confidence\": 0.35\n    },\n    {\n      \"id\": \"judged-dry-run-plan-omits-tip-commits\",\n      \"text\": \"The spec's Outcome prose promises 'Every reclaim-eligible LOCAL branch and its worktree (if any) is named with its tip commit', and AC-1's witness names tips for every listed branch — but dry-run output (report.go: KindEligible renders 'eligible: worktree \u003cpath\u003e + branch \u003cname\u003e') carries no tip; Row.Tip is populated only for KindReclaimed at --apply time (DeleteMergedBranch's pre-delete resolve). AC-2's operative dry-run sentence requires only path+branch, so the implementation conforms to the AC while the outcome prose overpromises; the recovery affordance exists only where recovery is actually needed (actual deletion). Matches ledger R4-I-82 adjudication (3), ACCEPTED-DEVIATION (prose-vs-AC looseness); restated because the in-repo living deviation-report is absent at this HEAD.\",\n      \"confidence\": 0.3\n    }\n  ]\n}\n```" }
frozen: { at: 2026-07-20, commit: e0e68e2bf37f2c309c0b6943e6d998bed40106a7 }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v0, inputs: [spec/gc-reclaim@e0e68e2bf37f2c309c0b6943e6d998bed40106a7, spec/gc-reclaim@2263d861657483b431e1e5bb2329169738056cc2], digest: sha256:162d6f4cc2606c0cead43fde182cc8a93dfc3570e4478feb5a3f98311fc3bd49, integrity: sha256:f60943b8efb9e26b34971797f0c47d4797fd953362e16203125e9eec4e0e5a1e, model: sha256:a8b2e3c1eb4defba461355077a2b82ae13db212beaad6e78eab05db51e5b6721 }
---
# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-judged-unmerged-branch-only-silently-omitted** [accepted-deviation]: AC-1 promises 'never a silent omission' and its witness promises the four unmerged close/* branches (attest-helper, close-preflight, disposition-verb, home-status-glance) each appear 'kept as unmerged' — but the implemented row model (predicate.go Compute, per dc-2) draws branch-only rows exclusively from residue.Result.MergedBranches, which residue.Scan pre-filters to merged branches only. An UNMERGED branch with no worktree therefore appears in neither row shape and is silently absent from both the dry-run plan and the --apply report — no 'kept: unmerged' line ever prints for it, contradicting AC-1's witness sentence. Structural, still present at HEAD e0e68e2; foreclosed in-story by co-2 (residue frozen) and dc-1 (no git re-derivation). Matches ledger R4-I-82 adjudication (1), ACCEPTED-DEVIATION with the AC-text inconsistency controller-owned; safety intact (unmerged is never touched, merely never listed). Restated here because the in-repo living deviation-report for spec/gc-reclaim does not yet exist at .verdi/specs/active/gc-reclaim/ on this HEAD, so this disclosure currently lives only in workspace PLAN-V1.md. (confidence 0.55) — ACCEPTED-DEVIATION per controller adjudication R4-I-82(1): AC-1's witness sentence promises 'kept: unmerged' lines for the four close/* branches, but the spec's own row model draws branch-only rows from residue's merged-only list -- the witness text contradicts the operative predicate two sentences earlier; the controller accepted that spec text at review, so the miss is the controller's, not this story's implementation. Fix is structurally foreclosed in-story: co-2 freezes internal/residue.Result byte-for-byte, and dc-1 forbids re-deriving eligibility from git independently. Safety intact: unmerged means never touched, merely never listed. Future work: a residue-v2 story exposing all-branch rows would make the disclosure buildable.
- **judged-judged-dc2-managed-step-widened-beyond-frozen-spec-text** [accepted-deviation]: The frozen spec's dc-2 defines the managed exclusion as exactly 'Managed false (else kept: managed)' — residue's own field, resolved against the invoking checkout's WorktreesRoot. The shipped predicate (predicate.go classifyWorktreeRow, commit c4a9122 / PR #191) instead tests 'wt.Managed || looksManagedAnywhere(wt.Path)', adding a path-segment heuristic (any '/.verdi/data/worktrees/' component, derived from wtmanager.WorktreesRoot) so a worktree managed by ANOTHER checkout is kept rather than reclaimed. This is the R4-I-82 FIX disposition and is safety-tightening, arguably truer to AC-1's intent ('verdi gc's existing managed-worktree jurisdiction') than dc-2's letter — but the frozen spec text was never amended, so dc-2's ordered switch and AC-1's literal 'eligible iff' predicate no longer describe the shipped classification (some rows AC-1's literal reading calls eligible are now kept: managed, and a user-placed unmanaged worktree whose path merely contains the segment is conservatively mislabeled 'managed'). Needs recording in the story's deviation-report or a ratified dc-2 amendment; the current in-code comment is the only in-repo disclosure. (confidence 0.35) — ACCEPTED-DEVIATION per controller adjudication R4-I-83: this divergence IS the R4-I-82(2) controller-adjudicated safety fix (PR #191's looksManagedAnywhere guard) -- its direction is strictly conservative, reclassifying rows eligible to kept only, never the reverse, so it cannot cause a deletion the frozen dc-2 text would forbid, only prevent one the frozen text would have wrongly allowed. Disclosed in code with citations to the finding and ledger; dc-2's frozen text remains accurate for the ordered structure and every other arm. Post-freeze divergence introduced by an adjudicated defect fix is the deviation mechanism's designed case; a spec amendment would be ceremony without safety value here.
- **judged-judged-dry-run-plan-omits-tip-commits** [accepted-deviation]: The spec's Outcome prose promises 'Every reclaim-eligible LOCAL branch and its worktree (if any) is named with its tip commit', and AC-1's witness names tips for every listed branch — but dry-run output (report.go: KindEligible renders 'eligible: worktree <path> + branch <name>') carries no tip; Row.Tip is populated only for KindReclaimed at --apply time (DeleteMergedBranch's pre-delete resolve). AC-2's operative dry-run sentence requires only path+branch, so the implementation conforms to the AC while the outcome prose overpromises; the recovery affordance exists only where recovery is actually needed (actual deletion). Matches ledger R4-I-82 adjudication (3), ACCEPTED-DEVIATION (prose-vs-AC looseness); restated because the in-repo living deviation-report is absent at this HEAD. (confidence 0.30) — ACCEPTED-DEVIATION per controller adjudication R4-I-82(3): the judge itself notes AC-2's own operative dry-run text (path+branch only) is conformant; the outcome clause's prose overpromised ('named with its tip commit' for every eligible item). Disclosed as prose-vs-AC looseness, not a predicate defect -- tips print on actual deletion (the --apply path), exactly where the recovery affordance is needed.
